title: Complete docs, examples, C emission, and regression coverage
goal: Finish the class/module design track by aligning docs, examples, interpreter behavior, generated-C behavior, and tests.
context: Run after the functional implementation tasks are complete or clearly documented as staged.
The user-facing docs should state what is implemented and what remains future work.
steps: Audit docs/CLASS_MODULE_DESIGN.md, README.md, docs/NAMING.md, docs/STDLIB.md, and ROADMAP.md for stale language.
Add final examples for dictionaries, sets, classes, modules, imports, aliases, inheritance, interfaces, and entry files as supported.
Ensure generated-C behavior is either implemented for the supported subset or explicitly rejected before emit with tests.
Run the full test suite and any relevant self-host gates if examples/manifests changed.
verification: sh scripts/selfhost_bootstrap_check.sh if self-host gates or manifests changed
done_when: The documented class/module subset matches actual behavior.
Examples and tests cover the completed feature track.
All CLASS tasks are passing or any remaining limitations are explicitly documented.
